overall_summary: Discussions centered on the ElizaOS v2.0.0 architecture and the upcoming elizacloud product launch, contrasted by significant community friction regarding token migration transparency and price performance.
key_facts: Odilitime proposed shipping ElizaOS v2.0.0 with zero default skills to prevent framework bloat, favoring a decentralized discovery model via external skills.md files.
The team scheduled the elizacloud product launch for the end of the current week or early next week to power the ElizaOS revenue flywheel.
Developer dinesh integrated support for over 100 onchain data sources into the EVM plugin using goldrush.dev.
A transparency gap was identified in the ai16z to elizaOS migration, with approximately 54% of the total supply reportedly unaccounted for.
The x402Guard security infrastructure was introduced to provide per-step transaction evaluation and layered limit systems for DeFi agents.
Community members reported an 'Invalid Dynamic Link' error in the Google Form-based role assignment system.
A proposal was made to create a centralized directory for skills and plugins modeled after Clawhub.
Effect AI proposed a native API integration to allow ElizaOS agents to outsource tasks to a decentralized human worker network.
open_questions: Does Milady support Sol or BSC after all?
What percentage of ai16z tokens actually migrated to elizaOS?
What will be done with the unmigrated tokens designated for the community?
Can I use Eliza to develop Solana dApps?
Should we ship v2.0.0 with 0 skills and promote external skill hosting/discovery?
Do you happen to have a ticket channel where I can talk to an admin?

new_issues_prs.title: v2.0.0 skills folder implementation
new_issues_prs.author: Odilitime
new_issues_prs.significance: Implements the core folder structure for the v2.0.0 skills architecture.

new_issues_prs.title: Major database refactor
new_issues_prs.author: unknown
new_issues_prs.significance: Improves long-term system architecture and reliability.

overall_focus.claim: Focus shifted toward a major beta release, prioritizing user onboarding, platform stability, and clearing functional blockers.
Development of decentralized identity features via the SAID Protocol on Solana for verifiable agent actions.

discord_updates.summary: Heavy debate regarding ELIZA token performance and the marketing strategy involving Shaw's promotion of Milady to drive elizacloud distribution.
Technical issues with role assignment forms and introduction of high-level AI/Full-stack developers to the community.
Discussion on establishing a structured registry system for skills and plugins similar to Clawhub.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Community frustration regarding the perceived lack of marketing and utility for the ELIZA token while competitors see growth.
Requirement for enterprise-grade UI trust signals and RTL localization (Arabic) to move agents from 'demo magic' to production ready.

strategic_insights.insight: Moving toward a zero-default-skill core for v2.0.0 shifts the discovery burden to the community and external domains.
implications_or_questions: Will this decrease barrier to entry for new users or create fragmentation?
How will the 'official' registry interact with these decentralized domains?

strategic_insights.insight: The high percentage of unmigrated tokens (est. 54%) creates a significant narrative risk and potential supply overhang concern.
implications_or_questions: Does the team have a deadline for the migration window?
What is the plan for the uncollected original supply?

market_analysis.observation: ELIZA token price performance is lagging behind the broader AI crypto ecosystem pump.
market_analysis.relevance: Drives community dissatisfaction and pressure on leadership for clearer utility roadmap.
